note: SEEDED KNOWN-BAD (P3, Constitution Rule 12 gate 2, caution.md C-035/C-089). An adjudicator model cites an IN-SET source_id (S1) but the quote it puts in quotation marks is PARAPHRASED - it is not a verbatim substring of S1. Models fabricate pincites and paraphrase inside quotation marks (Stanford HAI); on the old estate the corpus regex ran on raw HTML while the quote came from stripped text, so hit and quote disagreed (caution.md C-035). llm/gate.js validateResponse() must HARD-REJECT it with a quote_drift violation (verbatim re-match). The source_id is in-set and the schema is valid, so ONLY gate 2 can catch this: if the quote re-match is removed, the response is wrongly accepted and the calibration correctly fails. Run: node llm/gate.js --calibrate. A finding is emitted only when the poison is refused; zero findings fails eval/calibration-known-bad/run.js (earn-your-zero).
response: {"finding_id":"F-CK-102","verdict":"violation","source_id":"S1","quote":"we never use non-essential cookies without consent","rationale":"the site admits pre-consent tracking"}

sources.S1: We do not set any non-essential cookies until you have given your explicit consent through the banner.
sources.S2: You can withdraw your consent at any time from the cookie settings link in the footer.
